response:
Eleutherococcus is a genus of 38 species of thorny shrubs and trees in the family Araliaceae, native to eastern Asia, from southeast Siberia and Japan to the Philippines and Vietnam. They are commonly referred to as Siberian ginseng.

Amaryllis is the only genus in the subtribe Amaryllidinae (tribe Amaryllideae). It is a small genus of flowering bulbs, with two species. The better known of the two, "Amaryllis belladonna," is a native of the Western Cape region of South Africa, particularly the rocky southwest area between the Olifants River Valley to Knysna. Plants of the genus "Amaryllis" are known as belladonna lily, Jersey lily, naked lady, amarillo, Easter lily in Southern Australia or, in South Africa, March lily due to its propensity to flower around March.
